title: Bridge Networks, DNS, Ports, Routing, IPv6, and Exposure Policy · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares fresh bridge networks, dns, ports, routing, ipv6, and exposure policy decisions and accepts only answers tied to observable image, container, build, or Compose evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for least-exposure container network topology. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace container interfaces, network namespaces, veth pairs, bridge networks, routes, NAT, firewall rules, and platform boundaries.
Use user-defined networks, embedded DNS, service names, aliases, attach/detach behavior, and IP churn without hard-coded addresses.
Distinguish listening, EXPOSE, expose, publish, host address, host port, container port, and direct-routing behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge · changed case · Quiz Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Compose stack publishes its database on every host interface, pins a container IP, and calls localhost from the API. Split trust networks, use service DNS, restrict publishing, and verify ingress separately from egress.
